summary: Program-repair results support one bounded mechanism around patch tooling, while the other article claims remain comparative and benchmark-bound rather than causal.

bounded_claims.claim_text: No single agent framework leads across all three code-centric tasks.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence supports only a cross-task comparison of outcomes under the shared benchmark setup; it does not establish why task leadership changes across frameworks.

bounded_claims.claim_text: OpenHands leads software-development quality, but the strongest submetrics are split across frameworks.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence shows separation across quality, completeness, executability, and consistency, but it does not justify a causal explanation for why those submetrics split across systems.

bounded_claims.claim_text: GPTswarm leads overall vulnerability detection, but Gas Limitation vulnerabilities remain rarely detected across agents.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence supports a bounded performance claim that overall detection strength does not reliably extend to Gas Limitation cases; the reason for that weakness is not established here.
